# google_compute_global_vm_extension_policy

A Global VM Extension Policy.

~> **Warning:** This resource is in beta, and should be used with the terraform-provider-google-beta provider.
See [Provider Versions](../guides/provider_versions.html.markdown) for more details on beta resources.


<div class = "oics-button" style="float: right; margin: 0 0 -15px">
  <a href="https://console.cloud.google.com/cloudshell/open?cloudshell_git_repo=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Fterraform-google-modules%2Fdocs-examples.git&cloudshell_image=gcr.io%2Fcloudshell-images%2Fcloudshell%3Alatest&cloudshell_print=.%2Fmotd&cloudshell_tutorial=.%2Ftutorial.md&cloudshell_working_dir=compute_global_vm_extension_policy_basic&open_in_editor=main.tf" target="_blank">
    <img alt="Open in Cloud Shell" src="//gstatic.com/cloudssh/images/open-btn.svg" style="max-height: 44px; margin: 32px auto; max-width: 100%;">
  </a>
</div>
## Example Usage - Compute Global Vm Extension Policy Basic


```hcl
resource "google_compute_global_vm_extension_policy" "ops_agent_policy" {
  provider    = google-beta
  name        = "global-ops-agent-vme-policy-%{random_suffix}"
  description = "A basic global VM extension policy"
  priority    = 10

  extension_policies {
    extension_name = "ops-agent"
    pinned_version = "2.66.0"
  }

  instance_selectors {
    label_selector {
      inclusion_labels = {
        env = "test"
      }
    }
  }

  rollout_operation {
    rollout_input {
      predefined_rollout_plan = "FAST_ROLLOUT"
    }
  }
}
```

## Example Usage - Compute Global Vm Extension Policy Custom Rollout


```hcl
resource "google_compute_global_vm_extension_policy" "ops_agent_policy" {
  provider    = google-beta
  name        = "global-ops-agent-vme-policy-%{random_suffix}"
  description = "A global VM extension policy with a custom rollout plan"
  priority    = 10

  extension_policies {
    extension_name = "ops-agent"
    pinned_version = "2.66.0"
  }

  instance_selectors {
    label_selector {
      inclusion_labels = {
        env = "test"
      }
    }
  }

  rollout_operation {
    rollout_input {
      name = "projects/${data.google_project.project.number}/locations/global/rolloutPlans/${google_compute_rollout_plan.custom_rollout.name}"
    }
  }
}

data "google_project" "project" {
  provider = google-beta
}

resource "google_compute_rollout_plan" "custom_rollout" {
  provider       = google-beta
  name           = "custom-rollout-plan-%{random_suffix}"
  location_scope = "ZONAL"

  waves {
    display_name = "wave-1"
    selectors {
      location_selector {
        included_locations = [
          "us-central1-a",
          "us-west1-a"
        ]
      }
    }
    validation {
      type = "time"
      time_based_validation_metadata {
        wait_duration = "0s"
      }
    }
    orchestration_options {
      max_concurrent_resources_per_location = 10
      max_concurrent_locations              = 10
    }
  }
}
```

## Argument Reference

The following arguments are supported:


* `name` -
  (Required)
  Name of the resource. Provided by the client when the resource is created. The name must be 1-63 characters long and match the regular expression '^[a-z]([-a-z0-9]{0,61}[a-z0-9])?$' to comply with RFC1035.

* `extension_policies` -
  (Required)
  Map from extension (eg: "cloudops") to its policy configuration.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_extension_policies).

* `rollout_operation` -
  (Required)
  Represents the rollout operation.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_rollout_operation).


* `description` -
  (Optional)
  An optional description of this resource.

* `instance_selectors` -
  (Optional)
  Selector to target VMs for a policy.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_instance_selectors).

* `priority` -
  (Optional)
  Used to resolve conflicts when multiple policies are active. Defaults to 0.

* `project` - (Optional) The ID of the project in which the resource belongs.
    If it is not provided, the provider project is used.

* `deletion_policy` - (Optional) Whether Terraform will be prevented from destroying the resource. Defaults to DELETE.
	When a 'terraform destroy' or 'terraform apply' would delete the resource,
	the command will fail if this field is set to "PREVENT" in Terraform state.
	When set to "ABANDON", the command will remove the resource from Terraform
	management without updating or deleting the resource in the API.
	When set to "DELETE", deleting the resource is allowed.


<a name="nested_extension_policies"></a>The `extension_policies` block supports:

* `extension_name` - (Required) The identifier for this object. Format specified above.

* `pinned_version` -
  (Optional)
  The version pinning for the extension.

* `string_config` -
  (Optional)
  String configuration payload.

<a name="nested_rollout_operation"></a>The `rollout_operation` block supports:

* `rollout_input` -
  (Required)
  Rollout input settings.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_rollout_operation_rollout_input).

* `rollout_status` -
  (Output)
  Rollout status.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_rollout_operation_rollout_status).


<a name="nested_rollout_operation_rollout_input"></a>The `rollout_input` block supports:

* `conflict_behavior` -
  (Optional)
  Specifies the behavior of the rollout if a conflict is detected.

* `name` -
  (Optional)
  The name of the rollout plan.

* `predefined_rollout_plan` -
  (Optional)
  Specifies the predefined rollout plan for the policy.

* `retry_uuid` -
  (Optional)
  The UUID that identifies a policy rollout retry attempt. It should only be set when retrying an existing rollout. Updating this field along with other policy fields (description, extension_policies, instance_selectors, priority) in the same plan will return an error.

<a name="nested_rollout_operation_rollout_status"></a>The `rollout_status` block contains:

* `current_rollouts` -
  (Output)
  The current rollouts for the latest version of the resource.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_rollout_operation_rollout_status_current_rollouts).

* `previous_rollout` -
  (Output)
  Rollout status of the previous rollout.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_rollout_operation_rollout_status_previous_rollout).


<a name="nested_rollout_operation_rollout_status_current_rollouts"></a>The `current_rollouts` block contains:

* `location_rollout_status` -
  (Output)
  The rollout status for each location.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_rollout_operation_rollout_status_current_rollouts_location_rollout_status).

* `rollout` -
  (Output)
  The name of the rollout.

* `rollout_plan` -
  (Output)
  The name of the rollout plan.

* `state` -
  (Output)
  The overall state of the rollout.


<a name="nested_rollout_operation_rollout_status_current_rollouts_location_rollout_status"></a>The `location_rollout_status` block contains:

* `location_name` - (Required) The identifier for this object. Format specified above.

* `state` -
  (Output)
  The state of the location rollout.

<a name="nested_rollout_operation_rollout_status_previous_rollout"></a>The `previous_rollout` block contains:

* `location_rollout_status` -
  (Output)
  The rollout status for each location.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_rollout_operation_rollout_status_previous_rollout_location_rollout_status).

* `rollout` -
  (Output)
  The name of the rollout.

* `rollout_plan` -
  (Output)
  The name of the rollout plan.

* `state` -
  (Output)
  The overall state of the rollout.


<a name="nested_rollout_operation_rollout_status_previous_rollout_location_rollout_status"></a>The `location_rollout_status` block contains:

* `location_name` - (Required) The identifier for this object. Format specified above.

* `state` -
  (Output)
  The state of the location rollout.

<a name="nested_instance_selectors"></a>The `instance_selectors` block supports:

* `label_selector` -
  (Optional)
  LabelSelector matches VM labels.
  Structure is [documented below](#nested_instance_selectors_label_selector).


<a name="nested_instance_selectors_label_selector"></a>The `label_selector` block supports:

* `inclusion_labels` -
  (Optional)
  Labels as key value pairs.

## Attributes Reference

In addition to the arguments listed above, the following computed attributes are exported:

* `id` - an identifier for the resource with format `projects/{{project}}/global/vmExtensionPolicies/{{name}}`

* `scoped_resource_status` -
  The scoped resource status.

* `creation_timestamp` -
  Creation timestamp in RFC3339 text format.

* `id` -
  The unique identifier for the resource.

* `kind` -
  Type of the resource.

* `self_link` -
  Server-defined fully-qualified URL for this resource.

* `update_timestamp` -
  Update timestamp in RFC3339 text format.


## Timeouts

This resource provides the following
[Timeouts](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/plugin/sdkv2/resources/retries-and-customizable-timeouts) configuration options:

- `create` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `update` - Default is 20 minutes.
- `delete` - Default is 20 minutes.

## Import


GlobalVmExtensionPolicy can be imported using any of these accepted formats:

* `projects/{{project}}/global/vmExtensionPolicies/{{name}}`
* `{{project}}/{{name}}`
* `{{name}}`

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, use an [`identity` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/block/import#identity) to import GlobalVmExtensionPolicy using identity values. For example:

```tf
import {
  identity = {
    name = "<-required value->"
    project = "<-optional value->"
  }
  to = google_compute_global_vm_extension_policy.default
}
```

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import GlobalVmExtensionPolicy using one of the formats above. For example:

```tf
import {
  id = "projects/{{project}}/global/vmExtensionPolicies/{{name}}"
  to = google_compute_global_vm_extension_policy.default
}
```

When using the [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import), GlobalVmExtensionPolicy can be imported using one of the formats above. For example:

```
$ terraform import google_compute_global_vm_extension_policy.default projects/{{project}}/global/vmExtensionPolicies/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_compute_global_vm_extension_policy.default {{project}}/{{name}}
$ terraform import google_compute_global_vm_extension_policy.default {{name}}
```

## User Project Overrides

This resource supports [User Project Overrides](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/google/latest/docs/guides/provider_reference#user_project_override).
